RT by @WhiteHouse: VP Vance on @POTUS’s commitment to long term peace: 

“He’s thinking about this for my kids’ sake, for our grandkids’ sake, to ensure that you never have this regime with the ability to develop a nuclear weapon.”

For millions of people forced to flee war, conflict and persecution, protection can mean the difference between life and death.

Amid growing global challenges, the right to seek safety matters more than ever.

Saturday is #WorldRefugeeDay.

items.summary: President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva maintained his lead in polls on the Brazilian presidential election against Senator Flavio Bolsonaro, who is weighed down by his ties to a former banker involved in the country’s biggest bank fraud scandal.

items.title: With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

items.title: Iran reportedly closes Strait of Hormuz again, casting shadow over nuclear talks
items.summary: Iran's joint military command said the closure was in response to continued Israeli military operations in Lebanon.

Iran Sanctions Losing Efficacy
items.summary: In 2015, sanctions played a key role in bringing Iran to the negotiating table but now warfare has been the primary tool. Oliver Wyman Partner and Global Anti-Financial Crime Practice Leader Daniel Tannebaum explains to David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend how even if sanctions on Iran are lifted, businesses may remain hesitant to reengage with Iran due to lingering risks. items.title: New Strait of Hormuz Closure Announcement Threatens the Slow Uptick in Traffic
items.summary: Fifty-five ships had passed through the strait on Saturday, the U.S. military said. But then Iran’s military said it was closing the waterway once again.

items.title: Mideast Live Updates: Strait of Hormuz Re-emerges as Point of Tension After Iran Says It’s Closed
items.summary: Iran’s military command said it had closed the waterway over the continued fighting in Lebanon. But the U.S. military said traffic continued to flow. Still, the next phase of talks between Washington and Tehran appeared ready to start on Sunday.
